diagrams.title: Language Hierarchy in Computability Theory
diagrams.description: Shows containment relationship between language classes from regular to r.e.
elements: Arrows showing proper containment: Regular ⊂ CFL ⊂ Recursive ⊂ RE
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the strict containment hierarchy and understand that recursive ⊂ r.e.

diagrams.title: TM Decision Process Flowchart
diagrams.description: Flowchart showing how a TM decides or accepts a language
elements: Decision Point: Halts on all inputs? (Yes→Recursive, No→r.e.)
If Recursive: Accept if in L, Reject if not in L (both terminate)
If r.e.: Accept if in L; If not in L, either Reject or Loop forever
diagrams.purpose: Understand the key difference between recursive and r.e. languages - termination on rejection.

diagrams.title: Reduction Concept Diagram
diagrams.description: Illustration of how reduction proves undecidability
elements: Text: If B were decidable, apply f⁻¹ to solve A - contradiction!
diagrams.purpose: Understand reduction as a technique for proving undecidability by transforming known undecidable problems.
